growth_records.strain: environmental population, activated sludge from Schifflange wastewater treatment plant, Luxembourg (uncultured; no isolate strain)
growth_records.medium: activated sludge biomass amended with 13C-labelled substrates (in situ incubation)
growth_records.cite: Abdul R Sheik et al. In situ phenotypic heterogeneity among single cells of the filamentous bacterium Candidatus Microthrix parvicella. The ISME Journal (2016). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2015.181. [PMC5029219]
growth_records.snippet: In response to different substrates and temperature conditions tested in this study, the estimated Ca. M. parvicella growth rates ranged from 0.12 to 0.78 day-1, which are in agreement with those estimated using the total extended filament
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (filamentous actinobacterium) growth rates estimated at the single-cell level from newly assimilated 13C-oleic acid (Foster et al. 2011 approach), spanning the different substrate/temperature/oxygen conditions tested; reported as a range mu = 0.12-0.78 day-1. Converted to per-hour by /24: 0.12/24 = 0.005 h-1; 0.78/24 = 0.0325 h-1. Doubling times from td = ln2/mu: at 0.78 d-1, td = 0.693/0.78 = 0.888 d = 21.3 h; at 0.12 d-1, td = 0.693/0.12 = 5.78 d = 138.6 h. Authors note these agree with prior filament-length estimates (Tandoi et al. 1998; Rossetti et al. 2002). NO substrate uptake or product secretion rates in mmol/gDW/h are reported: single-cell assimilation is expressed only as 13C atomic percentage (relative enrichment) and as fraction of cells assimilating (13C-oleic acid: ~21-55% of cells; 13C-glycerol-3-phosphate: ~5-35% of cells; under alternating aerobic-anoxic: ~3-28% of cells), which are not convertible to metabolic-flux uptake rates. Bulk supernatant 13C substrate concentrations are in Supplementary Table S1 (not in main text). No MFA. Temperature-dependent assimilation showed two optima (activity at 4 C, decreasing to 20 C, rising 25-30 C, falling at 35 C); main incubations at 25 C. Medium is undefined activated-sludge matrix, not a defined medium.
